FROM maven:3.8-openjdk-8 AS builder

WORKDIR /home/app

COPY server/ /home/app/
RUN mvn clean package -DskipTests -Ppro

FROM eclipse-temurin:8-jre-focal

WORKDIR /app

COPY --from=builder /home/app/api/target/surveyking-*.jar /app/app.jar

EXPOSE 1991
RUN mkdir -p /app/files /app/logs

CMD ["java", "-jar", "/app/app.jar"]

file.content:
stages:
  - check
  - docker
  - deploy

# ==================== 测试服 (testserver 分支) ====================

# 检查 K8s 连接
check-testserver:
  tags:
    - "docker"
  stage: check
  image: 192.168.0.63:5000/px-infra-kubectl:latest
  only:
    - testserver
  script:
    - kubectl config view

# 构建并推送镜像到内网仓库
docker-testserver:
  tags:
    - "docker"
  stage: docker
  image: docker:19.03
  only:
    - testserver
  script:
    - docker build -f deploy/Dockerfile . -t 192.168.0.63:5000/px-surveyking-test:latest
    - docker push 192.168.0.63:5000/px-surveyking-test:latest
    - echo "镜像构建及推送完成"

# K8s 部署（首次 apply + 滚动更新）
deploy-testserver:
  tags:
    - "docker"
  stage: deploy
  image: 192.168.0.63:5000/px-infra-kubectl:latest
  only:
    refs:
      - testserver
  script:
    - kubectl rollout restart deployment surveyking-deploy -n surveyking

# ==================== 正式服 (master 分支) ====================

# 构建并推送镜像到 GCR
docker-master:
  tags:
    - docker
  stage: docker
  image: 192.168.0.63:5000/google/cloud-sdk:latest
  interruptible: true
  only:
    - master
  script:
    - gcloud auth activate-service-account --key-file ${GOOGLE_ONLINE_CONTAINER_REGISTRY}
    - gcloud auth configure-docker --quiet
    - docker build -f deploy/Dockerfile . -t us.gcr.io/mocan-cloud/px-surveyking:${CI_COMMIT_SHORT_SHA}
    - docker push us.gcr.io/mocan-cloud/px-surveyking:${CI_COMMIT_SHORT_SHA}
    - docker tag us.gcr.io/mocan-cloud/px-surveyking:${CI_COMMIT_SHORT_SHA} us.gcr.io/mocan-cloud/px-surveyking:latest
    - docker push us.gcr.io/mocan-cloud/px-surveyking:latest
    - echo "px-surveyking:${CI_COMMIT_SHORT_SHA} 推送成功"

# 部署留痕
deploy-master:
  tags:
    - xshell
  only:
    refs:
      - master
  stage: deploy
  variables:
    CACHE_DIR: "shell-runner-cache/surveyking-deployment"
    DIST_REPO: "https://oauth2:mTVn_6baAR8Koua-ZGcB@pt-gitlab.yottastudios.com/px/surveyking/deployment.git"
  script:
    - export CACHE_DIR=${HOME}/${CACHE_DIR}
    - if ! [ -e $CACHE_DIR ]; then git clone -q $DIST_REPO $CACHE_DIR; fi
    - cd $CACHE_DIR
    - git fetch -q --prune --tags "origin"
    - git checkout -B master remotes/origin/master
    - cd $CACHE_DIR
    - git config user.email "$GITLAB_USER_EMAIL"
    - git config user.name "$GITLAB_USER_NAME"
    - 'git commit --allow-empty -m "surveyking: $CI_COMMIT_MESSAGE (SHA: $CI_COMMIT_SHORT_SHA)"'
    - git push -q "origin" master:master
    - echo "部署留痕提交完成"
  dependencies:
    - docker-master

surveyking:apply-env:
  tags:
    - "docker"
  stage: apply-env
  image: 192.168.0.63:5000/px-infra-kubectl:latest
  only:
    refs:
      - master
    changes:
      - surveyking/.env
  script:
    - kubectl apply -f ./surveyking/namespace.yaml
    - kubectl create configmap surveyking-configmap --from-env-file=./surveyking/.env --dry-run=client -o yaml | kubectl apply -f - -n surveyking
    - |
      for file in $(find ./surveyking -name "*.yaml" ! -name ".gitlab-ci.yml"); do
        if grep -q "kind: Deployment" "$file" && grep -q "surveyking-configmap" "$file"; then
          DEPLOYMENT_NAME=$(grep -A2 "kind: Deployment" "$file" | grep "name:" | head -n1 | awk '{print $2}')
          if [ ! -z "$DEPLOYMENT_NAME" ]; then
            echo "重启使用configmap的deployment: $DEPLOYMENT_NAME"
            kubectl rollout restart deployment $DEPLOYMENT_NAME -n surveyking
          fi
        fi
      done

surveyking:deploy:
  tags:
    - "docker"
  stage: deploy
  image: 192.168.0.63:5000/px-infra-kubectl:latest
  only:
    refs:
      - master
    changes:
      - surveyking/**/*
  script:
    - cd ./surveyking
    - find ./ -maxdepth 1 -name "*.yaml" | sort | xargs -I {} kubectl apply -f {}

server:
  port: ${SERVER_PORT:18080}

spring:
  datasource:
    url: jdbc:mysql://${DB_HOST:localhost}:${DB_PORT:3306}/${DB_NAME:exam_system}?useUnicode=true&characterEncoding=utf-8&serverTimezone=Asia/Shanghai&useSSL=false&allowPublicKeyRetrieval=true
    username: ${DB_USERNAME:root}
    password: ${DB_PASSWORD:123456}
    driver-class-name: com.mysql.cj.jdbc.Driver

  security:
    oauth2:
      client:
        registration:
          default:
            client-id: ${OAUTH2_CLIENT_ID:exam-client}
            client-secret: ${OAUTH2_CLIENT_SECRET:exam-secret}
            authorization-grant-type: authorization_code
            redirect-uri: "{baseUrl}/login/oauth2/code/{registrationId}"
            scope: openid,profile,email
        provider:
          default:
            authorization-uri: ${OAUTH2_AUTH_URI:http://localhost:9000/oauth2/authorize}
            token-uri: ${OAUTH2_TOKEN_URI:http://localhost:9000/oauth2/token}
            user-info-uri: ${OAUTH2_USERINFO_URI:http://localhost:9000/userinfo}

# MyBatis-Plus
mybatis-plus:
  mapper-locations: classpath*:/mapper/**/*.xml
  configuration:
    map-underscore-to-camel-case: true
    log-impl: org.apache.ibatis.logging.nologging.NoLoggingImpl
  global-config:
    db-config:
      id-type: auto
      logic-delete-field: deleted
      logic-delete-value: 1
      logic-not-delete-value: 0

# JWT
jwt:
  secret: ${JWT_SECRET:YmFzZTY0LWVuY29kZWQtc2VjcmV0LWtleS1mb3ItZXhhbS1zeXN0ZW0tand0}
  expiration: ${JWT_EXPIRATION:86400000}

# 前端地址（OAuth2 回调重定向目标）
app:
  frontend-url: ${FRONTEND_URL:http://localhost:5173}

# CORS
cors:
  allowed-origins: ${CORS_ALLOWED_ORIGINS:http://localhost:5173,http://localhost:3000}

# Logging
logging:
  level:
    com.exam: debug

# ========== Stage 1: 构建前端 ==========
FROM node:20-alpine AS frontend-builder

WORKDIR /home/web

COPY exam-web/package.json exam-web/package-lock.json ./
RUN npm ci

COPY exam-web/ ./
RUN npm run build

# ========== Stage 2: 构建后端（含前端静态资源） ==========
FROM maven:3.9-eclipse-temurin-17 AS backend-builder

WORKDIR /home/app

COPY exam-system/ /home/app/
# 将前端构建产物拷贝到 Spring Boot 静态资源目录
COPY --from=frontend-builder /home/web/dist/ /home/app/src/main/resources/static/

RUN mvn clean package -DskipTests

# ========== Stage 3: 运行 ==========
FROM eclipse-temurin:17-jre-focal

WORKDIR /app

COPY --from=backend-builder /home/app/target/exam-system-*.jar /app/app.jar

EXPOSE 18080
RUN mkdir -p /app/files /app/logs

CMD ["java", "-jar", "/app/app.jar"]

stages:
  - check
  - docker
  - deploy

# ==================== 测试服 (testserver 分支) ====================

# 检查 K8s 连接
check-testserver:
  tags:
    - "docker"
  stage: check
  image: 192.168.0.63:5000/px-infra-kubectl:latest
  only:
    - testserver
  script:
    - kubectl config view

# 构建并推送镜像到内网仓库
docker-testserver:
  tags:
    - "docker"
  stage: docker
  image: docker:19.03
  only:
    - testserver
  script:
    - docker build -f deploy/Dockerfile . -t 192.168.0.63:5000/px-exam-system-test:latest
    - docker push 192.168.0.63:5000/px-exam-system-test:latest
    - echo "镜像构建及推送完成"

# K8s 部署（滚动更新）
deploy-testserver:
  tags:
    - "docker"
  stage: deploy
  image: 192.168.0.63:5000/px-infra-kubectl:latest
  only:
    refs:
      - testserver
  script:
    - kubectl rollout restart deployment exam-system-deploy -n exam-system

# ==================== 正式服 (master 分支) ====================

# 构建并推送镜像到 GCR
docker-master:
  tags:
    - docker
  stage: docker
  image: 192.168.0.63:5000/google/cloud-sdk:latest
  interruptible: true
  only:
    - master
  script:
    - gcloud auth activate-service-account --key-file ${GOOGLE_ONLINE_CONTAINER_REGISTRY}
    - gcloud auth configure-docker --quiet
    - docker build -f deploy/Dockerfile . -t us.gcr.io/mocan-cloud/px-exam-system:${CI_COMMIT_SHORT_SHA}
    - docker push us.gcr.io/mocan-cloud/px-exam-system:${CI_COMMIT_SHORT_SHA}
    - docker tag us.gcr.io/mocan-cloud/px-exam-system:${CI_COMMIT_SHORT_SHA} us.gcr.io/mocan-cloud/px-exam-system:latest
    - docker push us.gcr.io/mocan-cloud/px-exam-system:latest
    - echo "px-exam-system:${CI_COMMIT_SHORT_SHA} 推送成功"

# 部署留痕
deploy-master:
  tags:
    - xshell
  only:
    refs:
      - master
  stage: deploy
  variables:
    CACHE_DIR: "shell-runner-cache/exam-system-deployment"
    DIST_REPO: "https://oauth2:mTVn_6baAR8Koua-ZGcB@pt-gitlab.yottastudios.com/px/exams/deployment.git"
  script:
    - export CACHE_DIR=${HOME}/${CACHE_DIR}
    - if ! [ -e $CACHE_DIR ]; then git clone -q $DIST_REPO $CACHE_DIR; fi
    - cd $CACHE_DIR
    - git fetch -q --prune --tags "origin"
    - git checkout -B master remotes/origin/master
    - cd $CACHE_DIR
    - git config user.email "$GITLAB_USER_EMAIL"
    - git config user.name "$GITLAB_USER_NAME"
    - 'git commit --allow-empty -m "exam-system: $CI_COMMIT_MESSAGE (SHA: $CI_COMMIT_SHORT_SHA)"'
    - git push -q "origin" master:master
    - echo "部署留痕提交完成"
  dependencies:
    - docker-master

apiVersion: v1
kind: Namespace
metadata:
  name: exam-system

SERVER_PORT=18080
DB_HOST=mysql.default
DB_PORT=3306
DB_NAME=exam_system
DB_USERNAME=root
DB_PASSWORD=123456
JWT_SECRET=YmFzZTY0LWVuY29kZWQtc2VjcmV0LWtleS1mb3ItZXhhbS1zeXN0ZW0tand0
JWT_EXPIRATION=86400000
OAUTH2_CLIENT_ID=exam-client
OAUTH2_CLIENT_SECRET=exam-secret
OAUTH2_AUTH_URI=http://localhost:9000/oauth2/authorize
OAUTH2_TOKEN_URI=http://localhost:9000/oauth2/token
OAUTH2_USERINFO_URI=http://localhost:9000/userinfo
FRONTEND_URL=http://localhost:5173
CORS_ALLOWED_ORIGINS=*

apiVersion: v1
kind: PersistentVolume
metadata:
  name: exam-system-pv
spec:
  capacity:
    storage: 16Gi
  accessModes:
    - ReadWriteOnce
  hostPath:
    path: "/mnt/data/exam-system"
    type: DirectoryOrCreate
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: PersistentVolumeClaim
metadata:
  name: exam-system-pvc
  namespace: exam-system
spec:
  volumeName: exam-system-pv
  accessModes:
    - ReadWriteOnce
  resources:
    requests:
      storage: 16Gi

apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: exam-system-deploy
  namespace: exam-system
spec:
  replicas: 1
  revisionHistoryLimit: 3
  strategy:
    type: RollingUpdate
    rollingUpdate:
      maxUnavailable: 0
      maxSurge: 1
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: exam-system
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: exam-system
    spec:
      containers:
        - name: exam-system
          image: 192.168.0.63:5000/px-exam-system-test:latest
          imagePullPolicy: Always
          ports:
            - containerPort: 18080
          envFrom:
            - configMapRef:
                name: exam-system-configmap
          resources:
            requests:
              memory: "512Mi"
              cpu: "250m"
            limits:
              memory: "1Gi"
              cpu: "1000m"
          readinessProbe:
            tcpSocket:
              port: 18080
            initialDelaySeconds: 30
            periodSeconds: 10
            failureThreshold: 10
            timeoutSeconds: 3
          livenessProbe:
            tcpSocket:
              port: 18080
            initialDelaySeconds: 60
            periodSeconds: 30
            failureThreshold: 3
            timeoutSeconds: 3
          volumeMounts:
            - name: exam-system-data
              mountPath: /app/files
              subPath: files
            - name: exam-system-data
              mountPath: /app/logs
              subPath: logs
          lifecycle:
            preStop:
              exec:
                command: ["sh", "-c", "sleep 5"]
      volumes:
        - name: exam-system-data
          persistentVolumeClaim:
            claimName: exam-system-pvc
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: exam-system-svc
  namespace: exam-system
spec:
  type: NodePort
  ports:
    - port: 18080
      targetPort: 18080
      nodePort: 27287
      protocol: TCP
      name: http-port
  selector:
    app: exam-system

exam-system:apply-env:
  tags:
    - "docker"
  stage: apply-env
  image: 192.168.0.63:5000/px-infra-kubectl:latest
  only:
    refs:
      - master
    changes:
      - exam-system/.env
  script:
    - kubectl apply -f ./exam-system/namespace.yaml
    - kubectl create configmap exam-system-configmap --from-env-file=./exam-system/.env --dry-run=client -o yaml | kubectl apply -f - -n exam-system
    - |
      for file in $(find ./exam-system -name "*.yaml" ! -name ".gitlab-ci.yml"); do
        if grep -q "kind: Deployment" "$file" && grep -q "exam-system-configmap" "$file"; then
          DEPLOYMENT_NAME=$(grep -A2 "kind: Deployment" "$file" | grep "name:" | head -n1 | awk '{print $2}')
          if [ ! -z "$DEPLOYMENT_NAME" ]; then
            echo "重启使用configmap的deployment: $DEPLOYMENT_NAME"
            kubectl rollout restart deployment $DEPLOYMENT_NAME -n exam-system
          fi
        fi
      done

exam-system:deploy:
  tags:
    - "docker"
  stage: deploy
  image: 192.168.0.63:5000/px-infra-kubectl:latest
  only:
    refs:
      - master
    changes:
      - exam-system/**/*
  script:
    - cd ./exam-system
    - find ./ -maxdepth 1 -name "*.yaml" | sort | xargs -I {} kubectl apply -f {}
    - kubectl -n exam-system rollout restart deployment exam-system-deploy

      for file in $(find ./exam-system -name "*.yaml" ! -name ".gitlab-ci.yml"); do
        if grep -q "kind: Deployment" "$file" && grep -q "exam-system-configmap" "$file"; then
          DEPLOYMENT_NAME=$(grep -A2 "kind: Deployment" "$file" | grep "name:" | head -n1 | awk '{print $2}')
          if [ ! -z "$DEPLOYMENT_NAME" ]; then
            echo "重启使用configmap的deployment: $DEPLOYMENT_NAME"
            kubectl rollout restart deployment $DEPLOYMENT_NAME -n exam-system
          fi
        fi
      done

exam-system:deploy:
  tags:
    - "docker"
  stage: deploy
  image: 192.168.0.63:5000/px-infra-kubectl:latest
  only:
    refs:
      - master
    changes:
      - exam-system/**/*
  script:
    - cd ./exam-system
    - find ./ -maxdepth 1 -name "*.yaml" | sort | xargs -I {} kubectl apply -f {}
    - kubectl -n exam-system rollout restart deployment exam-system-deploy

搞完了！以下是这次改动的总结：

## 改动总结

### 创建的文件

| 文件 | 说明 |
|------|------|
| `deploy/Dockerfile` | 三阶段构建：① Node 20 构建前端 → ② Maven + JDK17 构建后端（前端 dist 拷到 `static/`）→ ③ JRE17 运行 |
| `.gitlab-ci.yml` | CI/CD 流水线，testserver 分支推内网镜像仓库 + K8s 滚动更新，master 分支推 GCR + 部署留痕 |
| `kubernetes/exam-system/namespace.yaml` | K8s 命名空间 `exam-system` |
| `kubernetes/exam-system/exam-system-deployment.yaml` | Deployment（1副本、滚动更新、健康检查）+ Service（NodePort 27287） |
| `kubernetes/exam-system/exam-system-pv.yaml` | PV/PVC 16Gi，挂载到 `/mnt/data/exam-system` |
| `kubernetes/exam-system/.env` | 环境变量配置（数据库、JWT、OAuth2 等） |
| `kubernetes/exam-system/.gitlab-ci.yml` | K8s 仓库的 CI（configmap 更新 + yaml apply） |

### 核心设计

- **前端打包进后端**：Dockerfile 多阶段构建，前端 `npm run build` 产物直接拷到 Spring Boot 的 `static/` 目录，最终只有一个 jar 包，一个容器搞定
- **完全对标参考项目**：CI/CD 流程、K8s 部署结构、镜像推送方式都跟 surveyking 保持一致

### 需要你后续注意的

1. `.gitlab-ci.yml` 中 master 分支的 `DIST_REPO` 地址我按参考项目的模式写了，你需要确认 GitLab 上是否有 `px/exams/deployment.git` 这个仓库，没有的话要建一个
2. `kubernetes/exam-system/.env` 里的 OAuth2 相关配置（`OAUTH2_AUTH_URI` 等）目前还是 localhost 占位，部署时需要改成实际地址
3. 前端构建时 Vite 默认输出到 `dist/` 目录，这跟 Dockerfile 里的路径是匹配的，不需要改 vite.config.ts

The user is setting up OAuth2 client credentials on a staging OA system for the exam project. They need to know what redirect URI to use.

Let me think about this:
- The exam system is deployed on testserver branch → internal K8s with NodePort 27287
- The OAuth2 callback in Spring Security is typically at `{baseUrl}/login/oauth2/code/{registrationId}`

file.content:
server:
  port: ${SERVER_PORT:18080}

spring:
  datasource:
    url: jdbc:mysql://${DB_HOST:localhost}:${DB_PORT:3306}/${DB_NAME:exam_system}?useUnicode=true&characterEncoding=utf-8&serverTimezone=Asia/Shanghai&useSSL=false&allowPublicKeyRetrieval=true
    username: ${DB_USERNAME:root}
    password: ${DB_PASSWORD:123456}
    driver-class-name: com.mysql.cj.jdbc.Driver

  security:
    oauth2:
      client:
        registration:
          default:
            client-id: ${OAUTH2_CLIENT_ID:exam-client}
            client-secret: ${OAUTH2_CLIENT_SECRET:exam-secret}
            authorization-grant-type: authorization_code
            redirect-uri: "{baseUrl}/login/oauth2/code/{registrationId}"
            scope: openid,profile,email
        provider:
          default:
            authorization-uri: ${OAUTH2_AUTH_URI:http://localhost:9000/oauth2/authorize}
            token-uri: ${OAUTH2_TOKEN_URI:http://localhost:9000/oauth2/token}
            user-info-uri: ${OAUTH2_USERINFO_URI:http://localhost:9000/userinfo}

# MyBatis-Plus
mybatis-plus:
  mapper-locations: classpath*:/mapper/**/*.xml
  configuration:
    map-underscore-to-camel-case: true
    log-impl: org.apache.ibatis.logging.nologging.NoLoggingImpl
  global-config:
    db-config:
      id-type: auto
      logic-delete-field: deleted
      logic-delete-value: 1
      logic-not-delete-value: 0

# JWT
jwt:
  secret: ${JWT_SECRET:YmFzZTY0LWVuY29kZWQtc2VjcmV0LWtleS1mb3ItZXhhbS1zeXN0ZW0tand0}
  expiration: ${JWT_EXPIRATION:86400000}

# 前端地址（OAuth2 回调重定向目标）
app:
  frontend-url: ${FRONTEND_URL:http://localhost:5173}

# CORS
cors:
  allowed-origins: ${CORS_ALLOWED_ORIGINS:http://localhost:5173,http://localhost:3000}

# Logging
logging:
  level:
    com.exam: debug

- 访问 `http://10.57.0.45:27287/` → Spring Boot 返回 `static/index.html`（前端页面）
- 前端请求 `/api/*` → 直接打到同一个后端（不需要代理了，同源）

message.content: 这样部署会不会有性能问题

content.text:
不会，这种方式很常见，没啥性能问题。简单说：

**前端打包后就是几个静态文件**（js/css/图片），Spring Boot 内嵌的 Tomcat 直接返回，跟 nginx 返回静态文件没本质区别。用户第一次加载后浏览器就缓存了，后续不再请求。

**真正的性能瓶颈永远在后端接口和数据库**，不在静态文件。

唯一的小差别是 Tomcat 处理静态文件确实不如 nginx 极致优化（gzip 压缩、304 缓存头等），但你这是内部考试系统，用户量有限，这点差别完全可以忽略。

如果以后真遇到性能瓶颈了，前面加个 nginx 反代就行，那是后话了。现在这么搞最省事，一个容器搞定，部署维护简单。

看到了，第60行那块"本地登录"没有做环境判断，直接写死显示了。需要根据环境变量控制。Vite 打包时可以通过 `import.meta.env.MODE` 或自定义环境变量来判断。

两个方案：

1. **前端判断**：用 Vite 环境变量 `VITE_DEV_LOGIN=true`，只在开发模式注入
2. **后端判断**：加个接口返回当前环境，前端调用后决定是否显示

方案1最简单，打包时不设置这个变量，dev 登录自然就不显示了。你选哪个？

content.text:
搞定。`import.meta.env.DEV` 在 `vite dev` 时为 `true`，`vite build` 后为 `false`。打包部署后"本地开发登录"那块直接不会渲染，而且 Vite 打包时会 tree-shaking 掉这段死代码，连 bundle 里都不会有。

**改动总结：** `login.tsx` 第60行，用 `import.meta.env.DEV` 包裹了 dev 登录区域，打包部署后自动隐藏。本地开发时还能正常用。

content.thinking:
The SecurityConfig looks fine - `/api/auth/oauth2/**` is permitted. The issue is likely that the Spring Security OAuth2 client auto-configuration is kicking in and causing problems. The application has `spring-boot-starter-security` and OAuth2 client config in application.yml, but the actual login flow is custom (not using Spring Security's built-in OAuth2 login).

Wait, looking at the pom.xml again - it has `spring-boot-starter-security` but not `spring-boot-starter-oauth2-client`. The OAuth2 config in application.yml is just being read as regular properties via `@Value`.
 
The 500 error might be stemming from Spring trying to auto-configure OAuth2 client based on those `spring.security.oauth2.client` properties in the YAML, even though the OAuth2 client dependency isn't explicitly listed - though it could be pulled in transitively. Since the properties are nested under `spring.security.oauth2.client.registration.default` and `spring.security.oauth2.client.provider.default`, Spring Boot's auto-configuration might attempt to initialize OAuth2 client support and fail if something's misconfigured or missing. The issue is likely being masked by a global exception handler that returns the generic 500 error format, so I need to find where that's defined to see the actual root cause. Without logs, the most probable culprits are OAuth2 auto-configuration failing, environment variables not being injected properly in the K8s deployment, or the authorization URI being null when the controller tries to use it.

content.thinking:
So the global exception handler catches all exceptions and returns the 500 with "系统内部错误". The actual error is logged at line 33. Without seeing the logs, I need to guess what could cause the 500 on the `/api/auth/oauth2/login` endpoint.

Looking at the AuthController's login method:
1. `isDevProfile()` - checks active profiles
2. `buildRedirectUri(request)` - builds redirect URI from request
3. `UriComponentsBuilder.fromHttpUrl(authorizationUri)` - constructs the authorize URL

The most likely issue: the `authorizationUri` value injection. In the .env, we set `OAUTH2_AUTH_URI=http://oa-core.info-staging.xinyoudi.com/oauth/authorize`. 

The AuthController injects the authorization URI through Spring's property resolution chain, which needs to map `spring.security.oauth2.client.provider.default.authorization-uri` to the environment variable. There could also be an OAuth2 auto-configuration issue if the starter dependency is included transitively—it might attempt to auto-configure OAuth2 login and fail due to incomplete configuration.

Looking at the pom.xml, I only see `spring-boot-starter-security` listed, not `spring-boot-starter-oauth2-client`, so the OAuth2 client auto-configuration classes shouldn't be on the classpath and those properties would just be ignored. But they'd still be available for `@Value` injection. The real issue might be simpler—maybe there's a bean creation error or the application didn't start properly in the first place.

Actually, the user mentioned they can see the login page, so the app is definitely running and serving static files. That rules out startup failures. The more likely culprit is that the environment variables aren't being properly injected into the ConfigMap, or the ConfigMap itself isn't being created correctly from the .env file. I'm realizing the OAuth2 client auto-configuration shouldn't even be active since `spring-boot-starter-oauth2-client` isn't a dependency—just `spring-boot-starter-security` is included. Without the actual container logs, I'm speculating too much; I need to ask the user to share what's actually failing so I can pinpoint the real issue.

Looking at the screenshot again, the endpoint is returning a JSON error response, which means the request reached the Spring Boot app and hit the GlobalExceptionHandler. The most likely culprit is a null or malformed property during injection—probably `authorizationUri` being null when the OAuth2 login flow tries to build the authorization URL.

I'm also considering whether the `scope` property might be causing issues, though it should be injected as a string and work fine with the query parameter. Without the actual pod logs, I should guide the user to check those first, but I can also suggest checking the `.env` file configuration to see if the OAuth2 properties are properly set.

Now I'm thinking about the DNS resolution - the staging hostname might not resolve from inside the cluster, but that would only matter when exchanging the code for a token, not when building the redirect URL. The login endpoint just constructs a URL string and returns a 302, so unless `UriComponentsBuilder.fromHttpUrl()` fails on a malformed URL, the redirect itself shouldn't fail. The `authorizationUri` should be valid, so that's unlikely to be the issue.

Actually, I'm wondering if Spring Boot's OAuth2 auto-configuration is trying to create beans based on the properties defined, and maybe that's causing the application context to fail during startup. With `spring.security.oauth2.client.registration.default` configured, Spring might be attempting to create a `ClientRegistration` bean that requires certain fields. Though if that were the case, the app probably wouldn't start at all - unless there's a lazy initialization issue happening. The quickest way to figure this out is to have the user check the application logs.

Looking at the logs, I can see:
1. "No active profile set, falling back to 1 default profile: "default"" - No active profile is set, so `isDevProfile()` returns false, meaning it won't try the dev login path.
2. The app started successfully.

But the important thing is - there are no error logs shown here for the actual 500 error. The `--tail=50` only shows startup logs. The user needs to reproduce the error and then check the logs again.

Wait, but maybe the error already happened and we need more logs, or the user needs to trigger the login again and then check logs.

Actually, let me look at the startup log more carefully. 

The configmap environment variables might not be getting injected properly - the "No active profile set" message suggests `SPRING_PROFILES_ACTIVE` isn't configured, though that's not necessarily a problem since we didn't set it in the .env file either. The real issue is I'm not seeing any error logs after startup, so the user should try clicking the OA login button again and then pull fresh logs to capture what's actually happening when the 500 error occurs.

Looking at the startup logs, the app initialized fine, so the 500 error must be triggered when the user clicks the login button. The login flow builds a redirect URI and constructs the authorization URL using the injected `authorizationUri` - but if those environment variables aren't actually being injected into the pod, that would explain the failure. The configmap was created, but the pod might need to be restarted after the configmap was deployed for the environment variables to take effect.

Wait, but the `@Value` annotations have default fallback values, so the app would still start even without the env vars. The real issue is figuring out what value `authorizationUri` actually received - if it fell back to the default `http://localhost:9000/oauth2/authorize`, that should still be a valid URL that wouldn't cause an exception. I need to look more carefully at where the exception is actually being thrown, since the error logs would show `[lbl][Exception][SYSTEM]` if there was a real error during the login request.

Actually, I'm realizing the 500 error screenshot the user showed was probably from before our recent changes - we've since fixed the TypeScript error and deployed again. The current startup logs don't show any errors, so I should ask the user to trigger the login flow again and check the logs with a larger tail count to see what's actually happening now.

content.thinking:
The URL is `http://10.57.0.45:27287/login` - this is a frontend route, not an API endpoint. The SPA (Single Page Application) needs all non-API routes to be handled by returning `index.html`. This is a classic SPA routing problem - when you refresh a page at `/login`, the server doesn't know how to handle it because it's a client-side route.

Spring Boot serves static files from `static/`, but when the user navigates to `/login`, there's no actual `login` file in the static directory. Spring Boot tries to find a resource and fails.

I need to add a controller or configuration that forwards all non-API, non-static-resource requests to `index.html`.

content.text:
经典的 SPA 路由问题。`/login` 是前端路由，不是后端接口。直接访问或刷新时，Spring Boot 找不到对应的静态文件就 500 了。

需要加一个转发配置，把非 API、非静态资源的请求都转发到 `index.html`。

The user wants me to continue. I was about to also handle the `NoResourceFoundException` for favicon.ico that was spamming logs. Let me also update the GlobalExceptionHandler to handle that, and make sure the SPA config doesn't interfere with `/api/**` routes.

Actually, let me also check if the SecurityConfig's `anyRequest().permitAll()` covers static resources properly. The SpaWebConfig should be fine as is. Let me just make sure the `/api/**` routes aren't caught by the SPA fallback.

Looking at the SpaWebConfig, the `PathResourceResolver` will match `/**` but since Spring MVC prioritizes more specific mappings (like `@RequestMapping("/api/**")`), API controllers will still be matched first. 

The SPA fallback only activates when no controller handles the request, so the API routes are safe. For the favicon.ico issue, the SPA config will return index.html when the file isn't found, which the browser will silently ignore since it expects an image—so that eliminates the 500 errors without needing special handling.

Now I should wrap this up and let them know they're ready to redeploy.

推代码重新部署就行。

content.text: CORS 配置的问题。`.env` 里 `CORS_ALLOWED_ORIGINS=*`，但代码里用了 `allowCredentials(true)`，Spring 不允许两者同时用。
前端打包进后端后是同源的，其实不需要 CORS 了。但为了兼容性，把 `*` 改成 `allowedOriginPatterns`：

registry.addMapping("/api/**")
                .allowedOrigins(allowedOrigins.split(","))
                .allowedMethods("GET", "POST", "PUT", "DELETE", "OPTIONS")
                .allowedHeaders("Authorization", "Content-Type")
                .allowCredentials(true);

content.thinking:
OK, two things happening:

1. The OAuth2 login redirect is working - it's redirecting to OA correctly.
2. The second error `MissingServletRequestParameterException: Required request parameter 'code'` - this means OA is calling back to `/api/auth/oauth2/callback` WITHOUT the `code` parameter. This could mean:
   - OA rejected the authorization and redirected back with an error instead of a code
   - Or the user denied the authorization
   - Or the OA passport client's redirect_uri doesn't match

Actually, looking more carefully, the redirect URL in the log is:
`http://oa-core.info-staging.xinyoudi.com/oauth/authorize?client_id=a1821114-9c93-4c52-9a8c-032449aba78b&redirect_uri=http://10.57.0.45:27287/api/auth/oauth2/callback&response_type=code&scope=openid,profile,email`


The missing `code` parameter in the callback confirms OA is rejecting the request - most likely because the passport client was set up with the default redirect URI instead of the one we're actually using. The mismatch between what OA expects and what we're sending means the user needs to recreate the passport client with the correct redirect URI.

Beyond that, the callback handler should also be defensive and handle cases where `code` is absent, since OA might return an error parameter instead.

kubectl rollout restart deployment exam-system-deploy -n exam-system
```

```

The user wants me to make the exam system compatible with OA's API. The issue is that when calling OA's `/api/user` endpoint with the access token, it returns 401. 

Looking at the code, the `fetchOaUserInfo` method uses `setBearerAuth(accessToken)` which sends `Authorization: Bearer xxx`. But Laravel Passport might expect the token in a different way, or the endpoint might be different.

Actually, looking at it again - the token exchange succeeded (we got an access_token), but the user info call failed with 401. This could be because:

1. The OA uses a different user info endpoint


2. The token type might not be "Bearer" - Laravel Passport usually uses Bearer tokens though
3. Maybe the OA's `/api/user` route doesn't use the `auth:api` guard, or uses a different middleware

I should check how other projects in the codebase integrate with OA to see what endpoint they're using for fetching user info. Let me look at the apitable or other projects to find a reference implementation. The user mentioned OA is working fine, so I just need to adjust the exam system to match whatever approach is already established elsewhere in the codebase.

The real issue could be that the `userInfoUri` endpoint is wrong - maybe OA uses `/api/v1/user` instead, or requires a different way of passing the token. I'm trying to figure out what the actual user info endpoint should be and how to properly authenticate with it, but without access to the other project codebases or OA's documentation, I need to think through the common failure points with Laravel Passport integrations.

The most likely culprits are that the access token isn't being passed correctly to the user info endpoint - either it needs to go as a query parameter instead of a Bearer header, or the endpoint itself doesn't exist at that path. Let me add some logging to see what token we actually got back from the exchange, and try passing it as a query parameter as a fallback since some Laravel apps support that through the TokenGuard. The token exchange worked fine, so the issue is likely with how we're sending the token to the user endpoint. I'm thinking the problem might be missing the `Accept: application/json` header—without it, Laravel might not properly validate the Bearer token in API requests. Let me add that header to the request.

content.text:
路由守卫逻辑没问题——`_layout.tsx` 的 `beforeLoad` 检查了 token，没有就跳 `/login`。

你现在看到的是 `localhost:5173/exams` 显示了内容，说明你的 `localStorage` 里之前存了 `exam_token`（可能是之前用 dev 登录存的）。所以前端认为你已登录，就直接渲染了页面，但后端没启动所以 API 调不通一直"加载中"。

**这不是 bug。** 清掉 localStorage 里的 `exam_token` 就会跳登录页了：

打开浏览器 F12 → Console → 输入：
```js
localStorage.removeItem("exam_token")
location.reload()
```

So the OA's token response already includes user info! We don't need to call `/api/user` separately. Let me check what fields `OAAuthTokenResp` has... Looking at the response structure, I can see it extracts the user info directly from the token response and validates it's not null before proceeding.

The OA's `/oauth/token` response contains user info nested inside it - the response probably looks like a JSON object with access_token, token_type, expires_in, and a nested user_info object containing email and nickname fields. This is why calling `/api/user` separately doesn't work - it's probably not an endpoint OA exposes for external OAuth clients. Instead, OA returns user info as part of the token response itself.

So the fix is to extract the user info directly from the token response rather than making a separate API call.

1→import { useQuery, useMutation, useQueryClient } from "@tanstack/react-query"
     2→import { get, post, del } from "@/lib/api"
     3→import type { User } from "@/types"
     4→
     5→const WHITELIST_KEY = "admin-whitelist"
     6→
     7→/** 获取白名单用户列表 */
     8→export function useWhitelist(examId: number) {
     9→  return useQuery<User[]>({
    10→    queryKey: [WHITELIST_KEY, examId],
    11→    queryFn: () => get<User[]>(`/api/admin/exams/${examId}/whitelist`),
    12→    enabled: examId > 0,
    13→  })
    14→}
    15→
    16→/** 添加白名单用户（按用户名） */
    17→export function useAddWhitelist() {
    18→  const queryClient = useQueryClient()
    19→  return useMutation({
    20→    mutationFn: ({ examId, usernames }: { examId: number; usernames: string[] }) =>
    21→      post<void>(`/api/admin/exams/${examId}/whitelist`, { usernames }),
    22→    onSuccess: (_data, variables) => {
    23→      queryClient.invalidateQueries({ queryKey: [WHITELIST_KEY, variables.examId] })
    24→    },
    25→  })
    26→}
    27→
    28→/** 移除白名单用户 */
    29→export function useRemoveWhitelist() {
    30→  const queryClient = useQueryClient()
    31→  return useMutation({
    32→    mutationFn: ({ examId, userId }: { examId: number; userId: number }) =>

content.thinking:
Now I can see the actual response. The `user_info` only contains `{nickname=扶苏, email=ceshi_fusu@yottastudios.com}` - there's NO `id` field! That's why `oaUserId` is null.

But looking at the JWT token, I can decode it. The `sub` field in the JWT is `85091` - that's the user ID!

So the access_token is a JWT, and the user ID is in the `sub` claim of the JWT. We need to either:
1. Parse the JWT to get the `sub` field as the user ID
2. Use `email` as the unique identifier instead of `id`


3. I'll go with email as the unique identifier since it's simpler and more reliable than decoding the JWT.

I also notice the whitelist issue - the user tried to add "冰糖" but the lookup is by username which is now email, so the whitelist needs to search by displayName as well.
